what_was_built: reports/crypto_d1_readiness_gate_v1/readiness_gate.json (the machine-readable readiness gate: 11 safety flags pinned False; research_only + read_only True; lane crypto_d1_protocol; BTC/ETH/SOL spot 1d 24/7; weekday-only filters forbidden; 10 audited Bundle 11-20 artifacts with on-disk path checks; 20 numbered readiness questions; readiness_status default NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA; 5 lifecycle options with per-status rules; 16 missing items; data_intake/qa_run/backtest future-authorization gates; trading_gate explicitly_forbidden=True; lane_recommendation pinned WATCH/MIXED with do_not_promote_to_ACTIVE/STRONG=True; safety_boundaries + no_profit_claim_policy carrying the 8 verbatim distinction phrases)

reports/crypto_d1_readiness_gate_v1/readiness_gate.md (human-readable readiness gate with disclaimer header and sections 0-10: what this gate is, lane+scope, audited-artifacts table, 20-question checklist, missing items, readiness status options + rules, why NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA is the honest default, what this gate does NOT authorize, allowed next steps, registry status, no-profit-claim policy)

tools/crypto_d1_readiness_gate_check.py (stdlib-only validator; argparse/json/pathlib only; validate + show subcommands; enforces schema, 11 false safety flags, 5 allowed lifecycle statuses with default NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA, scope pin (lane/assets/market/timeframe/calendar), 10 audited artifact ids with on-disk path-existence checks, exactly 20 readiness questions numbered 1..20 with Q20 == default status, missing_items non-empty, trading_gate forbidden, lane_recommendation WATCH/MIXED + no ACTIVE/STRONG promotion, 8 distinction phrases verbatim in BOTH md and json, no forbidden capability phrase in either doc)

tests/test_crypto_d1_readiness_gate.py (stdlib + pytest; existence + validation; eleven safety flags False; default-status pin; 5-option list pin; per-status rule presence; scope pin; 10 audited artifacts on disk; bundles 11..20 set; 20 questions numbered 1..20; Q19 paper/live locked YES; Q20 default status; non-empty missing items; blockers is list; trading_gate forbidden; intake/qa/backtest gate requirement lists non-empty; lane WATCH/MIXED + no ACTIVE/STRONG; 8 user-required verbatim phrases in MD; distinction phrases in both docs; no forbidden phrases; no data/crypto_d1_research/ dir; no real-data files in gate dir; validator failure-mode tests (tampered flag, wrong default status, unknown status, missing artifact, dropped question, promotion tamper, empty missing_items, forbidden phrase); validator stdlib-only; no network/broker/subprocess/env tokens; CLI validate+show smoke; 8 prior Crypto-D1 validators still pass; candidate registry validator still passes)
files_NOT_changed: tools/strategy_next_bundle.py (NOT modified -- no artificial selection nudge)
All prior Crypto-D1 spec docs (Bundles 11-20) + arbitrage validators NOT modified
data/crypto_d1_research/ NOT created (the gate is documentation; no data path is created)

scope_summary.pipeline_position: Capstone audit on top of Bundles 11-20; produces a single conservative readiness verdict (default NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA) and an explicit list of missing items. Adds no automation.

candidate_registry_status_after_build.note: The crypto_d1_protocol seed extra_files were extended with readiness_gate.md + readiness_gate.json for traceability only. The lane stays WATCH; evidence does NOT become STRONG; the candidate is NOT marked ACTIVE. Promotion to ACTIVE still requires real operator-supplied data + QA_PASS + a separately authorized backtest.

what_was_executed_in_this_bundle: ONLY the new validator (validate + show) and the test suite. NO real dataset was acquired, placed, QA-checked, or backtested. No data directory was created.
recommended_next_bundle: Operator step (NOT a bundle): follow the Bundle 20 runbook to choose a source class (A/B/C/D), acquire BTC/ETH/SOL daily-spot CSV offline, place files under data/crypto_d1_research/<dataset_id>/<dataset_version>/, author manifest.json + CHECKSUMS.txt + FREEZE_RECORD.txt + fees.json, then run `python tools/crypto_d1_data_qa_runtime_tool.py run --dataset-dir <PATH> --out-dir <PATH>` and review qa_report.json. SPARTA does NOT automate any of these steps.

Crypto-D1 Baseline Results Report v1 (separately authorized; only AFTER operator has supplied data, reached QA_PASS or accepted QA_WARN, and run the backtest runner to produce output; documents the operator-reviewed run + pins verdict + caveats. Spec bundle; Claude works.)

Crypto-D1 Backtest Runner v2 (future) -- walk-forward + per-family parameter sweep + structured sensitivity sweep + per-strategy report emitter matching the full Bundle 15 report schema. (Code bundle; recommend Codex.)
no_profit_claim_policy: A readiness gate is not strategy validation.
Specification completeness is not data readiness.
QA_PASS does not authorize automatic backtesting.
QA_PASS does not authorize paper trading.
QA_PASS does not authorize live trading.
No real data has entered SPARTA.
A good historical chart does not imply future returns.
